draft.introduction: Matthew 10:1–15 records Jesus commissioning the twelve apostles with authority over unclean spirits and disease. The delegation of divine power to human agents is central to Matthean theology. From Codex Sinaiticus.

translation.text: And he called his twelve disciples and gave them authority over unclean spirits, to cast them out, and to heal every disease and every affliction.
translation.original_text: και προσκαλεσαμενος τους δωδεκα μαθητας αυτου εδωκεν αυτοις εξουσιαν

translation.text: And as you go, preach, saying, “The kingdom of heaven has drawn near.” Heal the sick, raise the dead, cleanse lepers, cast out demons. Freely you received, freely give.
translation.original_text: πορευομενοι δε κηρυσσετε λεγοντες οτι ηγγικεν η βασιλεια των ουρανων

translator_notes.note: The term exousia (εξουσία), authority or power, is the same word used in John 1:12 for the power given to believers to become children of God. Here it is delegated from Christ to the apostles, establishing a chain of transmitted divine authority.
